variant: put CeraVe and La Roche-Posay moisturizers side by side in a table

request.message: put CeraVe and La Roche-Posay moisturizers side by side in a table

response_body.response: don't see specific product comparisons in your current docs. here's what i can pull together on both based on what's out there.  for your routine, both work in step 6 (moisturizer) after serums and actives. CeraVe AM lotion is lighter for morning; CeraVe PM cream or La Roche-Posay cicaplast for night if your skin is dry or compromised. if you're dealing with rosacea or sensitivity, La Roche-Posay's thermal water base has a slight edge for calming, but CeraVe's ceramide profile is more barrier-focused. which one are you leaning toward, or do you have a specific skin concern (acne, dryness, sensitivity)?

products.name: CeraVe PM Facial Moisturizing Lotion
products.description: Lightweight ceramide + niacinamide moisturizer for nightly use.

products.name: La Roche-Posay Cicaplast Baume B5
products.description: Healing balm with panthenol + madecassoside for compromised barrier.

products.name: The Ordinary Niacinamide 10% + Zinc 1%
products.description: 10% niacinamide for sebum control and pore appearance.

rows: thermal spring water, niacinamide, ceramides (varies by line)
lightweight to rich (depends on line: AM lotion vs PM cream)
lightweight to occlusive (toleriane, cicaplast, lipikar lines)
barrier repair, daily hydration, sensitive skin
cicaplast is heavy occlusive; toleriane is lighter
